source_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.




THE ROLE

We are hiring a SeniorTechnical Program Manager. This is not a coordination role or single-project ownership position. You will own the execution of multiple concurrent deployments and programs spanning battery energy storage systems, energy management software, hardware integrations, manufacturing, and customer projects running simultaneously at different stages.

You will be the person who sees around corners, surfaces blockers before they become problems, and holds the line on timelines without needing to be asked. You will work directly with engineering, field ops, and leadership.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own multiple concurrent BESS and utility scale projects at different lifecycle stages simultaneously

 - Maintain detailed program plans across all active projects including scope, schedule, budget tracking, risk registers, and milestone accountability

 - Coordinate across internal engineering, procurement, production, field ops, and logistics to sequence work correctly and surface interdependencies before they create delays

 - Own risk identification and mitigation across all active programs: track open issues, drive resolution, solve risk interdependencies and keep leadership informed without waiting to be asked

 - Manage external partners, subcontractors, and equipment vendors to contractual milestones, hold them accountable, and escalate when needed

 - Build and maintain the program infrastructure Critical Loop: source of truth process, tracking systems, reporting, success metrics (define & measure), milestone review cadences, and escalation paths

 - Translate complex technical and schedule realities into clear, accurate communication for customers, utility stakeholders, and internal leadership

 - Identify patterns across projects and feed learnings back into how the team scopes and plans future programs




MUST-HAVES

 - 10 Years of Experience owning multiple complex, customer-facing projects/ programs simultaneously from kickoff through delivery on timelines and can operate in ambiguity on compressed or accelerated deployment timelines .

 - Understands how to sequence work across engineering, procurement, operations, and external vendors.

 - Has owned customer and external partner relationships directly, including difficult conversations, running status meetings, and managing deliverable schedules.

 - Can hold a detailed program schedule with real dependency logic, not just a list of milestones, and knows how to recover when the schedule slips

 - Has managed external vendors, subcontractors, and equipment suppliers to contractual commitments and knows how to apply pressure without burning relationships

 - Comfortable operating in a hardware and field deployment context: understands commissioning sequences, site logistics, and what it actually takes to get a system energized

 - Can build a program structure from nothing and does not wait for process, tools, or a PMO to exist before creating them

 - Must be willing to travel up to 25% of the time


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Background in commercial energy, infrastructure, manufacturing, or

 - Experience managing programs that span both hardware development and field execution

 - Familiarity with supply chain constraints in a physical product company

 - Direct experience with utility interconnection processes, permission to operate timelines, or NTP-to-energization workflows

 - Background managing BESS projec

source_jobs.title: Lead Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.




LEAD RECRUITER

Critical Loop | Full-Time | Hybrid | Long Beach, CA

The Role

We are hiring our first dedicated Lead Recruiter.

You will own recruiting across the business while helping expand the recruiting infrastructure that supports Critical Loop as we scale.

You'll partner directly with hiring managers and executive leadership to attract exceptional talent across engineering, operations, manufacturing, corporate functions, and leadership. You'll also help build upon the existing recruiting processes, interview frameworks, and operational cadence that create a consistent, high-quality hiring experience.

You'll report directly to the People & Talent Lead and have the opportunity to continue to shape how recruiting operates at Critical Loop.

We're looking for someone who enjoys both sides of recruiting:

 - finding and closing exceptional talent

 - building better systems that make hiring easier tomorrow than it is today

What You'll Do

 - Own full-cycle recruiting across technical and non-technical roles, including sourcing, screening, interviewing, offer management, and candidate experience.

 - Partner with hiring managers to define hiring strategies, conduct intake meetings, build interview plans, and calibrate candidate profiles.

 - Build proactive talent pipelines for recurring and difficult-to-fill positions before hiring needs become urgent.

 - Develop sourcing strategies that leverage LinkedIn Recruiter, networking, referrals, communities, and creative outreach techniques.

 - Own recruiting operations within Ashby, including job creation, interview scheduling workflows, reporting, scorecards, templates, and process documentation.

 - Continuously improve interview processes, candidate communications, hiring manager enablement, and recruiting metrics.

 - Track recruiting analytics including time-to-fill, pipeline conversion, sourcing effectiveness, and hiring manager satisfaction.

 - Partner with the People & Talent Lead on onboarding improvements, employer initiatives, and People Operations projects that improve the employee experience.

 - Serve as a trusted advisor to hiring managers by challenging assumptions, improving interview quality, and helping teams make better hiring decisions.

 - Identify patterns across searches and recommend improvements to recruiting strategy, compensation positioning, interview design, and talent planning.

Must-Haves

 - 3+ years of full-cycle in-house recruiting experience across multiple business functions.

 - Demonstrated success sourcing passive candidates and building talent pipelines for difficult-to-fill positions.

 - Experience partnering directly with hiring managers throughout the hiring process, from intake through offer acceptance.

 - Strong interviewing and candidate assessment skills with the ability to evaluate both technical capability and long-term organizational fit.

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.

 - Highly organized with outstanding follow-through and attention to detail.

 - Comfortable managing multiple concurrent searches while maintaining an exceptional candidate experience.

 - Experience using modern ATS platforms (Ashby preferred, Greenhouse, Lever, or equivalent acceptable).

Nice-to-Haves

 - Experience recruiting for engineering, manufacturing, hardware, or operations organizations.

 - Experience working at a Series A, Series B, or other high-growth startup.

 - Experi

source_jobs.title: Test Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.




TEST ENGINEER - MULTIPLE OPENINGS - ON-SITE IN LONG BEACH, CA

The Role

We are hiring Test Engineers to build our hardware test capability from the ground up. You will be the owner of our hardware-in-the-loop (HITL) infrastructure, production unit test process, and canary fleet procedures, building and maintaining each as the hardware test pipeline our reliability story is built on.

You will design and build the HITL testbeds, stand up the production unit test process, and own the canary fleet procedures that our hardware reliability story is built on. The test outcomes you produce will feed directly back into the hardware we ship to customers. This is a hands-on, on-site role working alongside the Operations and Product Development teams.

What You'll Do

 - Design, build, and maintain hardware-in-the-loop (HITL) testbeds for Critical Loop's product line, starting with the CLB5100 and expanding to the CLB5020 and beyond

 - Develop and operate production unit test processes and procedures, ensuring issues are caught before units reach customer sites

 - Stand up and own canary fleet usage processes, tooling, and operational standards

 - Author and maintain test procedures, documentation, and standards as the source of truth for how Critical Loop validates hardware

 - Analyze test data and translate findings into actionable feedback that improves the hardware we ship

 - Lead root cause analysis on hardware failures and edge cases surfaced through testing, and stay with the problem until it is solved

 - Partner with engineering and field ops to align test infrastructure with deployment realities

 - Build the test engineering function from nothing and do not wait for process or tooling to exist before creating it

Must-Haves

 - Hands-on experience designing, building, and operating hardware test setups — not only running existing ones

 - Demonstrated experience authoring test procedures and the operational standards around them with substantive contributions to the build and methodology

 - Strong root cause analysis skills, with the ability to drive complex hardware issues to ground in systems you own end to end

 - Willingness to engage with the software side of testing, including reading code, writing scripts, and committing to git repositories

 - Bachelor's degree in electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - Comfortable operating as the test engineering function for a product line, setting your own priorities and managing your own work without an existing test organization to lean on

Nice-to-Haves

 - Direct experience with hardware-in-the-loop (HITL) testbeds and the trade-offs around fidelity, cost, and maintainability

 - Experience with data acquisition systems, sensors, DAQs, and logging or telemetry forwarding software

 - Familiarity with Python, git, and Linux

 - Background in power electronics or high-voltage systems

 - Background spanning both R&D and production environments

Critical Loop is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to building a diverse team and creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Sales Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.




REVENUE OPERATIONS MANAGER

Critical Loop | Full-Time | Hybrid, Long Beach, CA (remote considered for the right candidate)





THE ROLE

We are hiring our first dedicated Revenue Operations Manager. This is a net-new, foundational role, not a seat maintaining a system someone else built. You will own the tools, data, and processes that keep our revenue engine running as we scale: Salesforce, forecasting, commission tracking, and the full closed-won to cash flow.

Today this work sits with our Head of Revenue on top of selling and leading the team, and it has outgrown that arrangement. You will take it over end to end, report directly to the Head of Revenue with no layer in between and have a clean slate to build the RevOps function the way it should be built. You will work closely with Revenue, Product, Accounting, and Operations.

You will be the person who keeps our revenue data honest, our forecasts trustworthy, and our reps clear on exactly what they are earning.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own, manage, and roadmap Salesforce, including custom objects, flows, integrations, and dashboards that support accurate reporting on pWin, pipeline growth, segmentation, and revenue shape

 - Own the closed-won to invoice to collection process across sales, accounting, and the customer, and build a feedback loop that tightens cash conversion timing

 - Build and maintain a commission tracking system that gives both reps and accounting clear visibility into what is owed and why

 - Own revenue forecasting and reporting so leadership can rely on it for hiring and planning decisions

 - Maintain data integrity and pipeline hygiene across Salesforce, G-Drive, and the rest of the go-to-market stack

 - Partner across Revenue, Product, Accounting, and Operations to sequence work and surface issues before they slow the business down

 - Recommend and implement new tools and process as the company scales


MUST-HAVES

 - Deep, hands-on Salesforce expertise. You have personally built or rebuilt an org, not just maintained one someone else architected, and can speak to custom objects, validation rules, flows, integrations, and AppExchange decisions

 - Has owned or directly supported end-to-end Sales or Revenue Operations in a startup environment

 - A proven track record creating, managing, and iterating process from scratch, without waiting for tools or a playbook to exist

 - A self-starter who owns outcomes in ambiguity and can think on their feet

 - Comfortable holding forecasting, commission tracking, AR and cash conversion, and tooling at the same time


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience at a Seed to Series C company; tech or SaaS background preferred

 - Familiarity with Google Workspace, Atlassian, and Slack

 - Experience using AI tools such as Agentforce, Slack AI, Claude, or ChatGPT with a human in the loop, and good judgment about what to automate versus keep manual

 - Experience building or formalizing a RevOps function from the ground up


COMPENSATION

$110,000 to $135,000 base, plus an annual bonus target and equity.

Critical Loop is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to building a diverse team and creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteri

source_jobs.title: Materials Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.



The Role

We are hiring a Materials Coordinator to support our supply chain operations across receiving and purchasing. This is a hands-on role where your work directly impacts our builds. We will train you on our systems and processes. You do not need to arrive knowing BESS. You do need to arrive organized, dependable, and ready to move fast.

 

What You Will Do

Receiving and incoming inspection

 - Receive incoming shipments on the shop floor and perform a systematic check on every delivery: verify quantities against the purchase order, confirm part numbers and model numbers match, check for shipping damage on all packaging and equipment, and verify serial numbers and lot numbers where required

 - For high-value or complex items such as battery systems, transformers, inverters, and electrical assemblies, follow the inspection checklist and document the condition of each item with photos before moving it to storage

 - Identify and immediately escalate any discrepancy: short shipments, wrong parts, damaged goods, missing documentation, or serial number mismatches

 - Log all receipts accurately in our receiving system, including quantities received, condition notes, serial numbers, and lot numbers, and submit for manager approval before invoices are cleared

 - Complete and submit a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) for any damaged, missing, or non-conforming material, including photos and a clear written description of the issue

 - Keep parts location and on-hand quantity data accurate and current so the team always knows what is on site

Purchasing support

 - Support purchase order creation, tracking, and expediting across active projects, using correct cost codes and project codes for each build

 - Place orders with approved vendors and confirm pricing, lead times, and ship dates

 - Expedite open orders and flag late or at-risk parts before they affect the build schedule

 - Track open POs across multiple active projects and keep status current

 - Collect quotes and vendor documents as directed and organize them for review

Invoice processing

 - Perform a three-way match on every invoice: verify quantity, part number, and price against the purchase order and the receipt

 - Flag any invoice discrepancies to the appropriate team with supporting documentation

Must-Haves

 - Experience in purchasing, receiving, inventory, or materials coordination, preferably in a hardware, manufacturing, or industrial environment

 - Hands-on receiving experience: you have physically received shipments, verified them against a PO, and documented what you found

 - Knows how to catch a problem at the door: you can identify a short shipment, a wrong part, or shipping damage and you know what to do next

 - Comfortable working in spreadsheets and able to keep data clean, accurate, and organized

 - Strong attention to detail with part numbers, quantities, serial numbers, dates, and dollar amounts

 - Clear written communication for internal documentation, discrepancy reports, and escalations

 - Willing to work hands-on in a shop floor environment, including receiving and handling heavy or oversized electrical equipment

Nice-to-Haves

 - Experience with energy storage, batteries, electrical components, or industrial equipment

 - Familiarity with procurement or spend management software

 - Experience writing or processing Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) or similar discrepancy documentation

 - Exp

source_jobs.title: Electrician Technician (BESS Systems)
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.



We’re hiring hands-on Electrician Technicians to support critical field deployments across California. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys problem-solving in dynamic environments and wants consistent, project-based work with a growing team.

About the Role

We're looking for a hands-on Electrician Technician to own the electrical work across prototype buildouts, customer deployments, fleet servicing, and facility support for our battery energy storage systems (BESS) and mobile power conveyance equipment. This hybrid role combines field and headquarters-based work, including prototype development, engineering collaboration, and system preparation. The right person is safety-minded, technically sharp, proactive, and comfortable in a fast-moving startup setting where systems evolve and close collaboration with engineering is constant.



This is an on-site role based in our Long Beach office with up to 50% field travel.



Responsibilities:

 - Install, terminate, test, and commission BESS and mobile power conveyance systems during prototype builds and customer deployments.

 - Perform preventative maintenance, diagnostics, and repairs on low- and medium-voltage systems both in the field and at headquarters.

 - Ensure all work meets NFPA 70 (NEC), OSHA electrical safety standards, and permitting/inspection requirements; support inspection readiness.

 - Layout and wire panels, conduit, interconnects, and power infrastructure for deployed systems, prototypes, and facility installations.

 - Execute energized and de-energized work safely using proper PPE, lockout/tagout, and hazard analysis.

 - Collaborate closely with engineering and operations teams on prototype iterations, system updates, and continuous improvement initiatives.

 - Maintain clear records of inspections, issues, resolutions, and parts usage across field and facility operations.



Requirements:

 - Licensed Electrician (Journeyman or higher) or equivalent industrial/commercial experience.

 - 3+ years working with power systems, ideally including energy storage or mobile power equipment.

 - First hand knowledge of NFPA 70 (NEC), OSHA electrical safety, and coordination of permits/inspections.

 - Ability to read and interpret electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, and construction drawings.

 - Proficiency with standard electrical test tools (multimeter, insulation tester, clamp meter, etc.).

 - Comfortable performing both energized and de-energized work with correct safety protocols.

 - Willingness to travel up to 50% as needed.

 - Valid CA driver’s license.
   
   

Physical Requirements:

 - Ability to lift and move objects up to 50 lbs.

 - Ability to stand, stoop, bend, and kneel for extended periods during assembly, maintenance, and repairs.



Preferred Requirements:

 - Experience with inverters, DC infrastructure, and backup power systems (generators, UPS, ATS).

 - Familiarity with mobile or modular power platforms (skid- or trailer-mounted).

 - Commissioning and servicing of energy storage or renewable energy systems.

 - Knowledge of control wiring, sensors, and instrumentation.

 - Forklift certification or willingness to obtain.

 - C-10 contractor’s license.

 - Class A driver’s license.

Critical Loop is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to building a diverse team and creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. All qualified applicants will receive consideration fo

source_jobs.title: Senior Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.



The Role

We are hiring a Senior Mechanical Engineer to lead the structural, packaging, and thermal design of our high-power battery energy storage systems, power conversion systems, and ground support equipment. You will own mechanical problems end to end, from blank-paper concept through fabrication, validation, and field deployment.

This is a hands-on, on-site role. You will work alongside electrical engineers, field ops, and fabricators. You will not hand work off. You stay with the problem until it is solved.

What You Will Do

 - Lead the mechanical design of BESS, power conversion systems, and ground support equipment, including large welded and bolted structures for outdoor and on-road use

 - Own all project documentation, CAD models, mechanical drawings, BOMs, and work instructions

 - Work within a multidisciplinary environment to identify requirements and design systems that integrate cleanly with electrical and software teams

 - Work directly with fabricators and contract manufacturers to get hardware built and assembled to spec

 - Drive designs from blank paper through fabricated assembly, including managing schedules and tracking system design decisions

 - Identify and resolve design risks proactively, before they become field problems

 - Support field deployments and troubleshoot mechanical issues in the field when needed

 - Set your own priorities and manage your own workload without waiting for direction

Must-Haves

 - 10 or more years of experience with a BS in Mechanical Engineering or a related field with a mechanical engineering focus, from an accredited university

 - Strong ability to conduct structural analysis

 - Experience across at least 3 full product design cycles, including at least one as the responsible engineer owning design, BOM, and schedule tracking end to end

 - Ability to conduct basic thermal calculations

 - Proven ability to take a design from blank paper through fabricated assembly independently

 - Hands-on experience with measurement, assembly, and fabrication of hardware while managing requirements and outline design criteria

 - Knowledge of appropriate material selection

 - Comfortable functioning as the sole mechanical engineer on a project, setting priorities and managing your own work without a mechanical engineering group to lean on

 - 3 or more years with multiple 3D CAD systems; ability to work in SolidWorks and learn OnShape (no OnShape experience required, no certifications required)

 - 3 or more years generating mechanical drawings per ANSI standards (GD&T certification not required)

 - Able to ask questions, seek solutions, and operate effectively in a fast-paced R&D and build environment

Nice-to-Haves

 - Experience with assembly tooling, large weldments, ground support equipment, skids, trailers, lifting equipment, or other industrial hardware

 - 3 or more years in thermal and fluid design for air-cooled or liquid-cooled electronic systems

 - Experience with high voltage systems

 - Background in both R&D and production environments

 - Prior experience at an energy storage, EV, or industrial equipment company

 - Familiarity with manufacturing processes including sheet metal, welding, machining, and plastics

 - Finite Element Analysis, Computational Fluid Dynamics experience
   
   

Work Authorization We are not able to offer visa sponsorship for this role. Candidates must be authorized to work in the United

source_jobs.title: Senior Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

We’re looking for a Senior Electrical Engineer to help design and deploy next-generation energy systems at the intersection of power infrastructure, battery energy storage, and distributed energy resources (DERs). This role is ideal for someone who understands the intent behind electrical codes, not just the letter, and can thoughtfully navigate compliance while enabling innovation.

You’ll work in a fast-paced, highly collaborative environment where new ideas move quickly from concept to deployment. If you’re energized by solving complex power challenges and pushing technical boundaries responsibly, this is the role for you.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Lead electrical design and engineering for power systems, including battery energy storage systems (BESS), microgrids, and DER integrations

 - Design and review electrical distribution systems, including switchgear, panelboards, and custom electrical panels

 - Develop and review single-line diagrams, protection schemes, grounding plans, and equipment specifications

 - Engage directly with customers and partners to support technical discussions, project development, and issue resolution

 - Participate in site visits, commissioning activities, and field troubleshooting as needed

 - Ensure designs comply with NEC, local codes, UL standards, and utility requirements while identifying opportunities for creative, compliant solutions

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with product, manufacturing, and operations teams to bring projects from concept through commissioning

 - Interface with utilities, AHJs (Authorities Having Jurisdiction), and inspection bodies to support project approvals

 - Perform power system studies (load flow, short circuit, coordination, arc flash) and validate system performance

 - Stay current with evolving codes, standards, and emerging technologies in energy storage and distributed generation


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

Required

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering

 - 7+ years of experience in power systems, energy storage, renewables, or related fields

 - Strong working knowledge of the National Electrical Code (NEC) and its real-world application

 - Experience with battery energy storage systems (BESS), solar PV, microgrids, or other DERs

 - Hands-on experience with electrical panel design, including layout, component selection, and integration into larger power systems

 - Proficiency in power system analysis tools

 - Proficiency in industry-standard electrical CAD tools

 - Experience working with utilities and navigating interconnection processes

 - Demonstrated ability to develop engineering processes, standards, or best practices

 - Ability to interpret codes pragmatically and propose compliant, forward-thinking solutions

 - Willingness to travel (estimated 25%, depending on project needs)

Preferred

 - Active Professional Engineer (PE) license

 - Familiarity with UL 508A or similar panel standards

 - Strong communication skills and ability to engage effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

 


WHAT SETS YOU APART:

 - You understand not just what the code says, but why it exists—and when flexibility is appropriate

 - You bring both system-level thinking and hands-on design experience (down to the panel level)

 - You’ve helped build or scale engineering processes in a growing organization

 - You’re comfortable operating in ambiguity a

source_jobs.title: Operations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Critical Loop

Critical Loop is shaping the next generation of energy systems by building the integrated edge grid of the future. Specializing in advanced energy management and battery control systems, Critical Loop empowers businesses and communities to seamlessly unite distributed energy resources for faster, more reliable, and affordable grid access. With deep expertise in grid modernization and a passion for creating resilient, responsive energy solutions, Critical Loop drives innovation at the intersection of technology and energy.






OPERATIONS ENGINEER

Critical Loop | Full-Time | Multiple Openings




THE ROLE

We are hiring an Operations Engineer to lead the deployment, operation, maintenance, and performance of our mobile BESS fleet. You will ensure safe and reliable field operations, drive rapid customer response, and maintain high system uptime across active sites.

You will also work closely with our controls and software teams to test and validate advanced control logic across diverse power applications. This is a hands-on, customer-facing role with direct impact on daily operations and product evolution. If you need close oversight to function or treat safety as secondary to speed, this is not the right fit.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Manage fleet scheduling, dispatch, deployment, and retrieval to ensure reliable service delivery across active customer sites

 - Oversee site readiness, electrical connections, grounding, and system startup during deployments

 - Execute commissioning of battery energy storage and power systems in the field, safely verifying de-energized conditions before every touch

 - Develop and execute preventive and corrective maintenance plans to maximize fleet uptime

 - Lead hands-on troubleshooting of electrical, mechanical, battery, and power electronics issues independently using a structured diagnostic process

 - Serve as the primary escalation point for on-site and remote troubleshooting and customer support

 - Coordinate logistics, transportation, staging, and heavy equipment movement for deployments

 - Operate and test system control logic, state machines, and transitions between operating modes

 - Maintain accurate documentation on fleet status, maintenance activities, telemetry, and system performance

 - Communicate clearly with engineering teams on field findings and open issues, and with customers on status and resolution timelines

 - Provide on-call support in on-call rotation

 - Travel up to 50%


MUST-HAVES

 - Experience operating or supporting battery energy storage systems or similar power assets in the field

 - Hands-on experience with power electronics, electrical troubleshooting, and field maintenance

 - Has worked on 480V or higher systems and can safely verify de-energized conditions before every touch

 - Quality wiring, terminations, and ferrule work to a professional standard

 - Experience using backend or controls software to monitor and operate energy systems

 - Customer-facing experience and strong communication skills, including difficult conversations when systems are down or timelines slip

 - Comfortable managing work on a job site independently with minimal supervision

 - Familiarity with electrical safety practices and regulatory standards


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience with BESS, UPS, inverters, DC infrastructure, portable switchgear, or microgrid systems

 - Experience with mobile or portable power systems and fleet management

 - Basic coding experience or familiarity with Python, Julia, R, or MATLAB

 - Experience writing or modifying site configurations and defining state transitions for deployed systems

 - Familiarity with fleet management systems or telemetry platforms

 - Familiarity with Modbus or similar industrial control protocols

 - Prior background in a startup or fast-scaling operations environment

 - Certifications in battery systems, heavy equipment operation, or electrical safety

Critical Loop is an equal opportunity e

notes: Energy systems company with strong engineering focus
